column3: Research: do jmp condition-suffix mnemonics work? (Appendix B p.276 vs assembler error)
column13: Empirical research, no production change. Question: Appendix B p.276 says jmp takes the branch condition suffixes (jmpz/jmpn/...), but they error on assembly. Tested both tools: lccjs rejects jmpz/jmpn/jmpe with Invalid operation; oracle (cuh63 6.3) rejects jmpz IDENTICALLY — the leftover 7-byte .e is the deviation-10 blank artifact, not a real assembly (initial script misread the .e existence as acceptance; corrected). Static: assembler.js has no jmpXX cases, suffixes belong only to br; jmp encoding has no cc field. Conclusion: textbook DOC ERROR, not an lccjs gap — lccjs is parity-correct, no assembler fix. column3: Research: claim auto-fruit reuses a fruit whose agent session is still alive
column13: Spike confirming banana's root cause and recommending a fix. takenFruits() (scripts/claim.js) is worktree-scoped, not session-scoped: a fruit frees the instant its last worktree is removed, so an agent sitting between puzzles (alive, no worktree) is invisible and auto can hand its name to another agent. Key non-obvious finding: reconciling the code to its own header comment (scan all <fruit>/* branches instead of worktree list) would NOT fix it, because close deletes the branch too — only a session-lifetime artifact closes the gap. The design doc already listed 'fruit reuse after teardown' as an accepted edge case asserting 'never concurrently'; #193 is the counterexample that falsifies it (worktree-liveness != session-liveness). Options weighed: (A) session-sentinel branch — principled, keeps git as source of truth, recommended as deferred DEV puzzle if it recurs; (B) lockfile/registry — rejected, the design's explicit non-goal; (C) time-window — rejected, fragile; (D) mandatory --as for concurrent fan-out — recommended NOW at zero cost (already how the project operates). Deliverable: docs/research/claim-fruit-session-scope.md + an annotation on the design doc's edge-case bullet. No code shipped (severity:low, logging mitigation already exists). Proposed follow-ups: deferred DEV (Option A) + cheap DOCS (fix the claim.js header comment / design-doc source-of-truth claim). No marker (markdown-only research issue). Verified auto returns 'cherry' while apple+banana hold worktrees.

column3: Research: root-cause the pre-push pdd gate false-fail in worktrees → #224
column13: RESEARCH spike, reactive - triggered by the pre-push pdd gate false-failing while pushing PM #221's docs-only CSV row from a worktree. QUESTION: why does `npm run puzzles` fail on current_issues.md:9 from a .claude/worktrees/ path but pass on a clean checkout for a no-puzzle change? FINDING (root cause): pdd-0.24.2 compiles .pddignore excludes to regexps from the ABSOLUTE source path (Sources#fetch, sources.rb:23-28) and Glob#to_regexp (utils/glob.rb) emits literal path chars UNESCAPED (else-branch) - so the '+' that EnterWorktree puts in worktree dir names (<fruit>+<slug>) becomes a regex quantifier, silently no-opping ALL excludes -> pdd scans every markdown file -> trips on the literal TODO in the TODOS.md link at current_issues.md:9. Proven empirically (with + -> excludes match=false; without + -> true) and behaviorally (PASS from main checkout, FAIL from apple+pm-221). DELIVERABLE: filed #224 (bug, severity:medium) with repro + 3 fix paths (naming constraint / run-pdd.sh robustness / upstream Regexp.escape); cross-linked #205/#188/#194/#195 bidirectionally with per-issue context. DOGFOOD: this row was pushed from a worktree named WITHOUT a slash (apple-research-226, no + on disk), validating #224 fix #1 - npm run puzzles PASSED and the push needed NO --no-verify (contrast PM #221, which had to bypass from apple+pm-221). started_iso RECONSTRUCTED (~08:00, t0 not captured at the filing-turn start - recurring F1); finished_iso EXACT = 08:07:54, the GitHub timestamp of my last cross-link comment (#194), the final deliverable action - symptom-level diagnosis was front-loaded earlier in PM #221. h/c left EMPTY: reactive spike, no clean before-work forecast was captured. closed_commit empty per #186. column3: claim.js stale-main guard: refuse to stake from a local main behind origin/main

column13: ARC design rewrite of #222 (stays OPEN as the design ticket; NOT closed). column3: pre-push pdd scan voids .pddignore excludes from a worktree path with a regex-special char (forces --no-verify)
column13: DEV fix for the #224 pre-push pdd-gate false-fail. Root cause (pdd-0.24.2): Sources#fetch compiles each .pddignore exclude into a regexp from the ABSOLUTE source path, and Glob#to_regexp (utils/glob.rb) emits literal path chars verbatim (never Regexp.escape), so a regex-special char in the repo path turns each exclude into a non-matching regex -> ALL excludes silently void -> pdd scans docs/*.md and aborts on the first uppercase keyword. EnterWorktree names worktree dirs <fruit>+<slug>, and + is a quantifier, so the gate false-failed from every worktree, forcing --no-verify (which also skips the #205 conflict-marker gate). Fix #2 (in-our-control, durable): scripts/run-pdd.sh now detects a regex/glob metachar in the repo abs path (+ ( ) [ ] ^ $ | { } * ? \\ ; . is safely escaped by the gem) via a portable quoted-case per-char check (system grep is ugrep, parses bracket classes differently), and when unsafe scans through a special-char-free mktemp symlink; if even the temp path is unsafe it fails loudly instead of mis-scanning. Gate now correct from any worktree. New regression test tests/new/run-pdd.path-robust.e2e.spec.js (3 tests, self-skips when pdd absent like the oracle suites): + path with keyword only in excluded *.md passes via symlink (asserts the [run-pdd] note fired); real malformed marker in a scanned file still FAILS via symlink (gate not neutered); clean path passes without symlink. Verified the test fails against the pre-fix script (true regression). Doc note added to docs/claude_workflow.md PDD-scan section. Gotcha found: --skip-gitignore stays boolean true (TypeError [] + true) unless a .gitignore exists (bin/pdd:71-76) - scaffold adds one. Naming-constraint doc half stays in #195. Full primary suite green (692 pass). Single-commit close per #186. Worktree cherry/issue-224.

column3: Research/probe: does original LCC have a 300-char (or any) source-line length limit, and what does it count?
column13: RESEARCH probe of #244 (source-line length limit). LCC.js explicit 300-char raw-line cap (incl comments) is BY DESIGN and safer; raw-incl-comments counting confirmed correct (oracle buffer fills same way). column3: Research: LCC+ interpreter off-TTY stdin crash (setRawMode) — define the non-interactive stdin contract
column13: RESEARCH probe of #240 (interpreterplus crashes off-TTY at process.stdin.setRawMode). Findings doc docs/research/interpreterplus-off-tty-stdin-contract.md. Root cause: setRawMode is TTY-only; called unconditionally at TWO sites (main setup line 125, AND resetProcessStdin line 18) and the second is reached by FOUR paths (fatalExit incl. "cannot open file", the exit handler, the HALT trap, Ctrl-C) — so guarding only the issue-shown line 125 is insufficient. The non-interactive contract is already latent: nbain returns 0 ("no key") on an empty queue, so off-TTY the queue just never fills — no new semantics needed. Verified by a reverted prototype: with isTTY guards at both sites, write-only randDeterministic.ep runs to completion off-TTY and exits 0 (crashes today). Recommended baseline DEV puzzle: guard both sites + add an off-TTY write-only e2e smoke (the #198 determinism smoke that had to be abandoned). Secondary finding: cursor-control escapes ([?25h/[?25l/[H) also leak into piped output unguarded. Oracle NON-transferable: the LCC+ trap set (nbain/rand/sleep/bp/...) is lccjs-specific; cuh63 has no .ep/raw-mode model; only blocking sin/ain is shared and core interpreter already degrades via fs.readSync EOF. Open question routed to Charlie: desired off-TTY posture for the interactive nbain/bp game demos (accept non-terminating vs clean diagnostic vs scripted input) — does not block the baseline crash fix. No code shipped this row. Worktree banana-issue-240.

column3: Research: assembler author-name prompt on stdin blocks non-interactive .o production
column13: Clean-room diff LCC.js vs cuh63 oracle (fresh temp dir per run so no name.nnn cache masks the prompt; demos/m1.a as a .o-producing module). OG-LCC angle answered: oracle has NO --name flag, NO LCC_AUTHOR env, NO config for the author field; its only non-interactive mechanism is the cwd name.nnn cache, which LCC.js already mirrors faithfully (src/utils/name.js, cwd-relative by design). So the parity-correct non-interactive .o recipe already works: seed a name.nnn in the working dir. Recommendation for the Charlie design discussion: document name.nnn (no code change); optionally add an LCC.js-only LCC_AUTHOR/--name convenience (explicitly non-parity); avoid silent placeholder-on-EOF (diverges from oracle, bakes wrong author into .o). Surfaced 2 candidate follow-up parity deltas (NOT #241 scope): (A) non-atomic output -- LCC.js writeOutputFile() at assembler.js:555 runs BEFORE createNameFile() at :561, so a name-prompt failure leaves the .o on disk yet exits 1 while the oracle writes nothing; (B) success exit code -- LCC.js exits 0 on a good .o assemble, oracle exits 1. NOTE: opened this session by confabulating the issue as a fictional 30-byte section-name cap and writing a bogus research doc; caught it after re-reading the real issue, deleted the bad doc, restarted. column3: lccjs .string rejects n escape that the oracle accepts (Missing terminating quote)
column13:
RESEARCH probe of #157 (split from #150 sext). HEADLINE BUG DOES NOT REPRODUCE: lccjs .string "hi
" assembles+runs fine (
 -> newline); no Missing terminating quote. Escape handling correct since commit 8639411 (2024-12-30) ~17mo BEFORE the issue was filed (2026-05-29) - never a regression; #157's premise (the carried-over 
-rejection side-observation) was the misdiagnosis - NOT #150, which is a separate valid ticket about sext semantics. Shipped demos demoP.a/happy-path.a rely on 
 in .string, and pre-existing test #209 already pins escaped-newline - both disprove the headline. Q1: neither path fails for supported set; tokenizer escape flag (assembler.js:938) keeps escaped " from ending the string, parseString (:967) decodes; unknown escapes hit parseString default (:992) -> Unknown escape sequence, NEVER Missing terminating quote. Q3 (REAL divergence): lccjs supported set 
 	  \ " is byte-for-byte IDENTICAL to oracle; they differ ONLY on escapes outside that set - lccjs REJECTS with a clear error, oracle SILENTLY DROPS the backslash and keeps the literal char (\0->0, \a->a, \x41->x41, \101->101, \q->q; no C numeric/control escapes at all, verified by sout hexdump). Q2: two handlers not redundant (lexer flag vs decoder). Q4: no position dependence. Q5: demos rely on it. VERDICT: BY DESIGN, no lccjs code change - lccjs stricter (loud error vs silent backslash-drop) is safer; leniency diff not an oracle bug, NO report to Prof. Dos Reis. Deliverables: 3 regression tests in assembler.directives.integration.spec.js (describe .string escape sequences #157); docs/research/string-escape-parity.md (full parity table + Q1-Q5); core-behavior-matrix.md .string escape row; parity_deviations.md BY DESIGN #15 + changelog; portable probe public_experiments/string_escape_parity/. column3: DEV: implement scripts/close.js (npm run close) — racy push retry loop + verify-then-cleanup gate
column13: DEV impl of #266 (agent CHERRY), the tool from my own #242 design. scripts/close.js + npm run close, mirroring claim.js (sh/die/report helpers, pure seams, module.exports). Takes over AFTER the agent commits Closes #N — does NOT author the commit (boundary from design doc section 5). Pre-flight refuses unless: in a fruit/issue-N worktree branch matching N, HEAD message references Closes #N, no rebase/merge in progress, clean tree. Land loop: fetch/rebase/push up to --max (default 5); classifyPushError routes ok/race/rejected-other; a blocking (non-union) rebase conflict aborts + hands back, union-only conflict treated as a config bug. Gate: shouldCleanup({onOriginMain}) — teardown ONLY if git branch -r --contains HEAD includes origin/main (the single chokepoint making cleanup-after-failed-push structurally impossible). Best-effort gh issue-state recheck. KEY FIX during dev: the #200 incident message literally contains [remote rejected] yet IS the race to retry — so classifyPushError checks definitively-fatal reasons (auth/protected/GH006/hook) FIRST, then race reasons (cannot lock ref/non-ff/fetch first/behind), trusts success only after, defaults unknown->rejected-other (no blind looping). 9 push cases + shouldCleanup + classifyRebaseConflict smoke-pass; full suite 44 suites/715 green; dry-run pre-flight correctly refuses with no close commit. Full unit+e2e coverage is sibling #267. Closed via the interim manual &&-gated chain (close.js not yet self-hosting on its first commit). Single-commit close per #186; closed_commit empty. column3: RESEARCH: should lcc.js's link path optionally use the in-memory linkObjectModules seam? (deferred / low-pri)
column13: Spike Q1-Q4 on routing lcc.js link path through linkObjectModules seam. Findings: Q1 no concrete beneficiary (browser api.js is single-module assemble->run, never links; showcase uses same API; no in-process assemble->link->run path; linkerplus.js absent). Q2 moving file I/O + progress logs into lcc.js grows it ~16->30 lines, cannot delete link() (standalone linker.js needs it), violates orchestration-only rule. Q3 seam is silent (silent=true); rewrite risks oracle-parity drift on Linking/Creating progress lines + exit-0 behavior. Q4 net negative. Recommendation: CLOSE AS YAGNI. Findings comment posted to issue. Research-only, no repo change; close decision left to reporter.

column13: Probed real Oracle ($LCC_ORACLE) -d debugger via scripted stdin + lccrun.sh. KEY: the prior "c crashes oracle" hearsay is imprecise — c r0 5 (register form) WORKS (r0->0005); only BARE c (no operands) segfaults (exit 139). c r0 (missing val)=clean Missing operand. integer-n, z, t, w<addr> all WORK. w<label>/c<label> on bare .e = Undefined label (symbol table absent on .e, probe artifact). Doc: docs/research/oracle-debugger-change-value-probe.md w/ verbatim transcripts. Implications: #1349 fix = IMPLEMENT c<reg><val>+integer-n in lccjs (with input validation, dont replicate bare-c segfault), not de-advertise; z/w/t are real Oracle cmds = legit parity targets; W8 upstream report WARRANTED (bare-c segfault). actual_min EMPTY: inter-turn idle.

column3: DEV: data-driven ilcc command registry + generated help + collision/parity/coverage guards
column13: Replaced the if/startsWith dispatch chain in iinterpreter.runInteractive() with a data-driven COMMAND_REGISTRY (module-level array, mirrors _instructionTable idiom; each entry {key,match,helpLabel,help,helpExtra,provenance,test,run->effect}). Dispatch iterates registry; matchCommand() encodes precedence (exact/prefix before numeric; 0 stays exact). displayHelp() GENERATED from registry, byte-identical to the original (HELP_ORDER restores reader-friendly order). validateCommandRegistry() throws at module load on duplicate key (collision guard). New tests/new/command-registry.spec.js: 3 guards (collision incl throw-on-dup, parity help<->dispatch no-orphans, coverage every entry has resolving test or pending(#1343)) + matchCommand precedence sanity. c/l/<enter> marked pending(#1343).
